summary: Backend bug-fix validation for POST /api/food/analyze. The `preview: bool = False` field was successfully added to FoodImageAnalyzeRequest (line 3219 of server.py). All 9 pytest tests passed 100%.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix confirmed: `preview: bool = False` is present at line 3219 of server.py inside FoodImageAnalyzeRequest. The original AttributeError is gone.
Endpoint correctly checks `if not request.preview:` at line 7745 before calling db.food_logs.insert_one().
seed_data_creation: TEST_Grilled Chicken log entry created and cleaned; TEST_preview_* user IDs used for isolation
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix iteration: FoodImageAnalyzeRequest was missing `preview: bool = False`. Fix applied and verified. POST /api/food/analyze with preview=true returns 200 OK without DB insert; preview=false inserts normally. /food/log endpoint also verified working with DB persistence.
rca_of_issue: Root Cause: `FoodImageAnalyzeRequest` model (defined at line 3213) was missing the `preview` field. The endpoint code at line 7745 referenced `request.preview` which caused `AttributeError: 'FoodImageAnalyzeRequest' object has no attribute 'preview'` → 500 Internal Server Error. Fix: Added `preview: bool = False` at line 3219 of server.py. Verification: All 9 test cases pass including (a) preview=True returns 200 with no DB row inserted, (b) preview=False/omitted inserts correctly, (c) short image still returns 400 from guard clause, (d) /food/log CRUD confirmed working.
